label: Post topic
helper: Enter the product, idea, announcement, story, event, or subject.
why: The model cannot safely determine the main subject without user input.
display: Always visible

label: Primary goal
helper: Choose the single most important result the post should support.
why: The goal determines the post angle, structure, emphasis, and CTA.
display: Always visible

label: Target audience
placeholder: Who should this post speak to?
helper: Describe the intended reader, customer, follower, or community.
why: Audience context materially changes vocabulary, examples, benefits, and tone.
display: Always visible

label: Core message
placeholder: What should people understand or remember?
helper: State the main takeaway rather than several competing messages.
why: It establishes the central idea around which the caption must be built.
display: Always visible

label: Essential details
placeholder: Add important names, dates, features, terms, or context.
helper: Include details that must appear accurately in the post.
why: It prevents the model from guessing important factual information.
display: Always visible

label: Output language
helper: Enter the language in which the complete post package should be written.
why: The publishing language cannot always be inferred from the topic or interface.
display: Always visible

label: Content mode
helper: Choose a mode or let the model infer it from the brief.
why: Different Instagram post types require meaningfully different structures and voices.
display: Always visible

label: Caption length
helper: Platform appropriate chooses the shortest length that communicates the message well.
why: Desired depth cannot always be inferred from the topic alone.
display: Always visible

label: Desired action
helper: Choose one reader action or let the model select a suitable CTA.
why: It controls the conversion action without requiring the user to write CTA copy.
display: Always visible

label: Supporting points
placeholder: Add benefits, examples, steps, objections, or secondary points.
helper: Include only points that strengthen the main message.
why: It allows richer posts without overloading the main Core message field.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Offer details
placeholder: Describe the offer, price, availability, dates, and terms.
helper: Use exact confirmed information and leave unknown details out.
why: Promotional posts require exact terms that the model must not invent.
display: Show when `contentMode` is “Offer or promotion” or `primaryGoal` is “Promote an offer.”

label: Approved facts and proof
placeholder: Add verified figures, results, quotes, credentials, or evidence.
helper: Only include information approved for public use.
why: It enables evidence-based claims while preventing fabricated proof.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Required disclosures
placeholder: Add sponsorship, affiliate, gifted-product, or other disclosures.
helper: Enter wording that must be visible in or near the caption.
why: Commercial relationships and regulated contexts may require explicit disclosure.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Additional requirements
placeholder: Add restrictions, required wording, exclusions, or other instructions.
helper: Use this for anything important not covered by the other fields.
why: It provides one controlled location for exceptional requirements without duplicating fields.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Brand voice
placeholder: Describe how the account normally sounds.
helper: Add a short voice description or examples of preferred phrasing.
why: A distinctive existing voice cannot be reliably inferred from the topic.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Tone
helper: Choose the closest overall tone; the model will keep it natural.
why: Tone materially changes sentence rhythm, vocabulary, and emotional intensity.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Emoji use
helper: Controls frequency without requiring the user to choose individual emojis.
why: Emoji preferences vary significantly between accounts and audiences.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Hashtag strategy
helper: Controls whether and how hashtags are included.
why: Hashtag use is an account-level preference that cannot always be inferred.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Provided hashtags
helper: Only these hashtags will be considered when Provided only is selected.
why: It supports controlled campaigns and approved brand hashtags without inventing alternatives.
display: Show when `hashtagPreference` is “Provided only.”

label: Visual format
helper: Choose the available asset type or let the model recommend one.
why: Visual recommendations should match the asset the user can realistically publish.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Visual details or asset
placeholder: Describe the image, asset, setting, subject, or visual restrictions.
helper: Explain what is available or what the visual should communicate.
why: The model needs asset-specific context to avoid suggesting an unrelated visual.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Alt text
helper: Auto includes alt text when the visual adds meaningful information.
why: It gives users control over accessibility output without forcing an extra field.
display: Always available in the collapsed group

label: Number of variants
helper: Generate one to three complete versions with different angles.
why: Some users need alternatives, but generating many versions by default would add noise.
display: Always available in the collapsed group
